mechanism_narrative: Low nominal interest rates reduce the opportunity cost of holding equities and compress equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 1%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0% signals deteriorating household income and collapsing consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the signal's Rank IC is positive over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales and housing starts reveal broad-based demand collapse that corporate earnings cannot withstand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of joint-down periods, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis monetary easing regime, industrial production growth signals successful policy transmission to real activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 52% of easing-regime observations when INDPRO rises, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production forces downward earnings revisions across the cyclical sector, dragging the S&P 500 lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns following month-over-month INDPRO declines in more than 50% of cases, or the Rank IC is positive over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth directly tracks aggregate nominal corporate revenue growth, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when output expands.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth coincides with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth signals rising labor income that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth coincides with negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts signal durable goods demand and construction employment strength that lifts broad corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when HOUST exceeds 400, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is negative over a 3-year rolling window.
